# criblio_search_source (Resource)

Ingest source for Local Search: listen address, optional auth tokens, TLS, and health metadata (`/search/local_search/sources`).

**Terraform provisioning:** Creating many `criblio_search_source` resources in parallel in a single apply is not supported by the control plane. Create sources **one at a time** (for example with `depends_on` between resources). Allow **at least 60 seconds** between successive **create** operations in the same workspace (for example using [`time_sleep`](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/time/latest/docs/resources/sleep) between dependents).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_search_source" "my_searchsource" {
  auth_tokens = [
    {
      description = "...my_description..."
      enabled     = false
      token       = "...my_token..."
    }
  ]
  cribl_api       = "/cribl/_bulk"
  description     = "...my_description..."
  disabled        = false
  elastic_api     = "/elastic/_bulk"
  host            = "...my_host..."
  id              = "...my_id..."
  port            = 8088
  prometheus_api  = "/write"
  splunk_hec_acks = true
  splunk_hec_api  = "/services/collector"
  subscriptions = [
    {
      batch_timeout      = 5
      content_format     = "RenderedText"
      heartbeat_interval = 60
      id                 = "default-subscription"
      queries = [
        {
          path             = "Security"
          query_expression = "*"
        }
      ]
      query_selector    = "simple"
      subscription_name = "subscription-1"
      targets = [
      ]
      version   = "7f0c2f2e-1c3b-4d2a-9d6e-5a1b2c3d4e5f"
      xml_query = "//Event/System"
    }
  ]
  tcp_port = 36722
  tls = {
    cert_path     = "...my_cert_path..."
    disabled      = true
    min_version   = "TLSv1.1"
    priv_key_path = "...my_priv_key_path..."
  }
  type     = "splunk_hec"
  udp_port = 38619
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host` (String) Bind address for the listener.
- `id` (String) Source configuration id.
- `type` (String) Protocol discriminator selecting the ingest source protocol and listener shape. possible known values include one of ["cribl_http", "datadog_agent", "elastic", "http_raw", "open_telemetry", "prometheus_rw", "splunk", "splunk_hec", "syslog", "tcp", "tcpjson", "wef", "wiz_webhook"]

### Optional

- `auth_tokens` (Attributes List) Optional bearer or shared tokens accepted by this source. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--auth_tokens))
- `cribl_api` (String) Absolute path for Cribl HTTP API requests (for example <code>/cribl/_bulk</code>). Used when <code>type</code> is <code>cribl_http</code>.
- `description` (String) Optional description shown in configuration UIs.
- `disabled` (Boolean) When true, the source does not accept new connections.
- `elastic_api` (String) Absolute path for Elasticsearch bulk API requests. Used when <code>type</code> is <code>elastic</code> (for example <code>/elastic/_bulk</code>).
- `port` (Number) TCP port when the source type uses a single port field. Must be a valid listener port (1–65535). Each port may be used by at most one Local Search source at a time for the same host and protocol: the same port cannot be shared across different sources or different <code>type</code> values. In Cribl Cloud, the UI only lists ports from the workspace pre-provisioned pool (the set varies by environment; Splunk HEC commonly defaults to 8088). Ports already in use by another source, or outside the pool, are rejected by the API—pick an available port from the UI or API.
- `prometheus_api` (String) Absolute path for Prometheus remote write requests. Used when <code>type</code> is <code>prometheus_rw</code> (for example <code>/write</code>).
- `splunk_hec_acks` (Boolean) When true, enable Splunk HEC acknowledgements. Used when <code>type</code> is <code>splunk_hec</code>.
- `splunk_hec_api` (String) Absolute path for Splunk HTTP Event Collector (HEC) API requests. Required when <code>type</code> is <code>splunk_hec</code> (for example <code>/services/collector</code>).
- `subscriptions` (Attributes List) WEF subscriptions; required when <code>type</code> is <code>wef</code>. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subscriptions))
- `tcp_port` (Number) TCP listen port when the source type uses a dedicated TCP port (for example <code>syslog</code>).
- `tls` (Attributes) TLS listener settings for Local Search sources (paths often use cloud substitution tokens). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tls))
- `udp_port` (Number) UDP listen port when applicable (for example <code>syslog</code> or <code>prometheus_rw</code> when required by the deployment).

<a id="nestedatt--auth_tokens"></a>
### Nested Schema for `auth_tokens`

Optional:

- `description` (String) Optional label for this token.
- `enabled` (Boolean) When false, the token is rejected.
- `token` (String) Secret token string clients must present. Not Null


<a id="nestedatt--subscriptions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscriptions`

Optional:

- `batch_timeout` (Number) Seconds to collect events before sending to Cribl. Not Null
- `content_format` (String) Content format for delivered events. possible known values include one of ["Raw", "RenderedText"]; Not Null
- `heartbeat_interval` (Number) Maximum seconds between endpoint check-ins before marking unavailable. Not Null
- `id` (String)
- `queries` (Attributes List) Path and XPath selector entries when using simple mode. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subscriptions--queries))
- `query_selector` (String) Query builder mode; simple with queries, or xml with xmlQuery. possible known values include one of ["simple", "xml"]
- `subscription_name` (String) Subscription name exposed to Windows clients. Not Null
- `targets` (List of String) DNS names of endpoints that forward these events (wildcards allowed). Not Null
- `version` (String) Version UUID for this subscription; changes when subscription parameters change.
- `xml_query` (String) XPath query when using xml mode.

<a id="nestedatt--subscriptions--queries"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscriptions.queries`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path attribute from the relevant XML Select element. Not Null
- `query_expression` (String) XPath query inside the relevant XML Select element. Not Null



<a id="nestedatt--tls"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tls`

Optional:

- `cert_path` (String) Filesystem or cloud path to the certificate PEM.
- `disabled` (Boolean) When true, TLS is not terminated on this listener. Not Null
- `min_version` (String) possible known values include one of ["TLSv1.3", "TLSv1.2", "TLSv1.1", "TLSv1"]
- `priv_key_path` (String) Filesystem or cloud path to the private key PEM.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_search_source.my_criblio_search_source
  id = "..."
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_search_source.my_criblio_search_source "..."
```
